title: Make replay leases region-safe
summary: Review the unchanged atomic claim contract first, then confirm database time governs lease creation and concurrent workers cannot disagree.

chapters.title: Preserve atomic ownership
chapters.summary: The schema and upsert still provide one ownership boundary for every failed delivery.

stops.title: Keep lease recovery inside the upsert
stops.summary: The expiry predicate remains the only path for replacing an existing replay owner.

chapters.title: Use one clock across regions
chapters.summary: The handler now obtains the lease timestamp from Postgres before attempting the claim.

stops.title: Create leases from database time
stops.summary: Confirm application clock drift can no longer change the effective lease window.

stops.title: Exercise skewed regional clocks
stops.summary: The regression case proves two workers still agree on ownership despite different process clocks.

support.title: Replay claim schema
support.reason: The primary key and lease columns are unchanged by the clock-source follow-up.

support.title: Operator claim status
support.reason: The existing operations state continues to expose the active owner and lease expiry.
